summary: Joel Spolsky briefly promotes Jessica Livingston's book 'Founders at Work,' a collection of interviews with 32 high-tech company founders. He notes that Livingston, a Y Combinator partner, has posted some chapters online including an interview with him that tells the early Fog Creek Software story. He shares a quote from his interview about his motivation for starting Fog Creek: seeing incompetent people succeed and figuring he could do at least as well. The post is essentially a short book recommendation with a humorous jab at Amazon's shipping speed.
key_points: Jessica Livingston's 'Founders at Work' contains interviews with 32 high-tech founders, including Joel Spolsky on the origins of Fog Creek Software
Joel's founding motivation was observing less competent people achieving high valuations and reasoning he could match or exceed their success
The book is positioned as essential reading for anyone starting a tech company
money_quotes: There's a bunch of people out there doing certain types of things and they seem to be pretty incompetent, but they're getting huge valuations.
Surely if I did those same things, knowing that I am less incompetent—merely semi-incompetent as opposed to extremely incompetent—I should be able to achieve at least their level of success.
Virtually everyone starting a high tech company will be all over this book like senators on cake.
key_insight: A viable founding motivation is simply observing that less competent people are succeeding and reasoning that you can do at least as well.
